title: Construction operatives n.e.c.
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate, usually through subordinate supervisory personnel, activities concerned with the construction and maintenance of structures, facilities, and systems. Participate in the conceptual development of a construction project and oversee its organization, scheduling, budgeting, and implementation. Includes managers in specialized construction fields, such as carpentry or plumbing. Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. Perform engineering duties in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of building structures and facilities, such as roads, railroads, airports, bridges, harbors, channels, dams, irrigation projects, pipelines, power plants, and water and sewage systems. (Based on 78 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Install, repair, or replace single-ply roofing systems using waterproof sheet materials.
Cover exposed nailheads with roofing cement or caulking to prevent water leakage or rust.
Mop or pour hot asphalt or tar onto roof bases or apply alternate layers of hot asphalt or tar and roofing paper to roofs.
Spray roofs, sidings, or walls to bind, seal, insulate, or soundproof sections of structures.
Remove snow, water, or debris from roofs prior to applying roofing materials; inspect problem roofs to determine the best repair procedures.
Cover roofs or exterior walls of structures with slate, asphalt, aluminum, wood, gravel, gypsum, or related materials.
Set up scaffolding and provide safe access to roofs for roofing tasks.
Estimate materials and labor required to complete roofing jobs.
Install attic ventilation systems and skylights on roofs to improve energy efficiency.
Apply reflective roof coatings to reduce solar heat absorption.
Install layers of vegetation-based green roofs, including protective membranes and drainage materials.
Apply insulation to building exteriors by installing prefabricated insulation systems or covering outer walls with insulation board.
Apply adhesive cement to floor or wall material to join and adhere foundation materials.
Cut covering and foundation materials according to blueprints and diagrams for construction work.
Remove existing plaster, drywall, or paneling using hand tools.
Supervise crew members and assist in training them for various construction tasks.
Prepare reports and maintain records of services rendered and tools used.
Spray acoustic materials or texture finish over walls or ceilings.
Install guide wires on exterior surfaces of buildings to indicate thickness of plaster or stucco and nail wire mesh to hold stucco in place.
Apply weatherproof, decorative coverings to exterior surfaces of buildings by troweling or spraying on coats of stucco.
Weigh clay, mix with water and chemicals to make drilling mud, using portable mixers.
String cables through pulleys and blocks, and steady pipes during connection to or disconnection from drill or casing strings.
Measure and cut openings in panels or tiles for electrical outlets, windows, vents, plumbing, or other fixtures using keyhole saws or cutting tools.
Inspect furrings, mechanical mountings, or masonry surfaces for plumbness and level, using spirit or water levels.
Cut and screw together metal channels to make floor or ceiling frames according to plans for the location of rooms or hallways.
Trim rough edges from wallboard to maintain even joints, using knives, and assemble or install metal framing or decorative trim for windows, doorways, or vents.
Hang drywall panels on metal frameworks of walls and ceilings in buildings using lifts or hoists when necessary.
Listen to engines or other equipment to detect faulty operations or unusual conditions, and fit and fasten wallboard or drywall into position on frameworks.
Read blueprints or specifications to determine installation methods, work procedures, or material tool requirements, and measure and mark surfaces for layout.
Clean up before and after installation, including vacuuming carpet and discarding remnant pieces, and maintain and perform safety inspections on equipment and tools.
Drive truck-mounted units to well sites and unload and assemble pipes and pumping equipment using hand tools.
Mix mortar and plaster to desired consistency or direct workers who perform mixing.
Prepare cost and labor estimates based on calculations of time and materials needed for projects.
Dig trenches to desired or required depths by hand or using trenching tools, and level trenches using tamping machines or hand tools.
Operate mechanized equipment such as pickup trucks, rollers, front-end loaders, or backhoes.
Read blueprints, specifications, or building codes to determine job requirements and review work details or procedures.
Install or repair sanitary or stormwater sewer structures or pipe systems and connect pipe pieces and seal joints using welding equipment or glue.
Tend machines that pump concrete, grout, cement, sand, plaster, or stucco through spray guns for application to ceilings or walls.
Clean and renew steam traps, and clean work areas, machines, or equipment to maintain a clean and safe job site.
Immerse pipe in chemical solution to remove dirt, oil, and scale.
Install gas burners to convert furnaces from wood, coal, or oil, under the direction of a carpenter.
Select tools, equipment, or materials from storage and transport items to work site; requisition tools and equipment as needed.
Position and hold timbers, lumber, or paneling in place for fastening, and fasten timbers or lumber with glue, screws, pegs, or nails.
Hold plumb bobs, sighting rods, or other equipment to aid in establishing reference points and lines, and align, straighten, plumb or square forms for installation.
Smooth or sand surfaces to remove ridges, tool marks, glue, or caulking, and examine electrical units for loose connections and broken insulation.
Perform tie spacing layout, measure, mark, drill or cut, and secure stakes to grids for construction of footings, nail scabs to footing forms, and vibrate and float concrete.
Construct forms and assist in raising them to the required elevation, and monitor how the wind, heat, or cold affect the curing of concrete.
Spread, level, and smooth concrete, using various tools, and signal truck driver to position truck for pouring concrete effectively.
Drill holes and pull or push wiring through openings, and lay out dimensions and outlines on stone prior to cutting.
Install and seal air ducts, and recommend energy conservation measures, such as the use of low flow showerheads and energy-efficient lighting.
Repair leaks in plumbing or gas lines as needed, and disassemble defective electrical equipment for repair, replacing worn parts as necessary.
Inspect buildings to identify required weatherization measures, including repair or replacement needs.
Test operation of plumbing fixtures after connecting water hoses to plumbing systems, and keep records of work assignments and tasks completed.
Discuss fencing needs with customers, estimate pricing, and measure designated amounts of ingredients for construction material mixes.
Clean mold-contaminated sites by removing damaged porous materials and thoroughly cleaning contaminated nonporous materials.
Install various types of bolts, including truss, glue, and resin bolts, traversing entire ceiling spans, and test bolts for specified tension.
Perform ventilation tasks such as hanging ventilation curtains and tubes; dust rocks following bolting completion.
Conduct inspections of equipment operation to detect binding or stoppage, and reassemble equipment when repairs are complete.
Determine amount of air leakage in buildings, using a blower door machine.
Comply with prescribed safety procedures or national laws regulating waste disposal methods.
Drive trucks or other heavy equipment to convey contaminated waste to designated locations.
Operate machines or equipment to remove, package, store, or transport loads of waste materials.
Load or unload materials into containers or onto trucks, using hoists or forklifts.
Identify hazardous materials to be removed, using monitoring devices.
Assemble and maintain physical structures, using hand or power tools.
Transfer tools, parts, equipment, and supplies to and from work stations and other areas.
Perform routine maintenance, such as inspecting drives, motors, or belts, checking fluid levels, or replacing filters.
Dismantle machines, equipment, or devices to access and remove defective parts, using hand tools or power tools.
Inspect or review projects to monitor compliance with building and safety codes or other regulations.
Plan, organize, or direct activities concerned with the construction or maintenance of structures, facilities, or systems.

Regulate air pressure, rotary speed, and downward pressure according to the type of rock or concrete being drilled.
Operate water-well drilling rigs and other equipment to drill, bore, and dig for water wells or for environmental assessment purposes.
Record drilling progress and geological data and document geological formations encountered during work.
Drive trucks or truck-mounted drills to and from work sites, and operate machines to flush earth cuttings from holes.
Perform routine maintenance and upgrade work on machines and equipment, such as replacing parts and lubricating machinery.
Select the appropriate drill for the job, and verify depths and alignments of boring positions.
Design water or wastewater treatment systems and oversee the construction of decentralized or on-site treatment systems.
Analyze the efficiency of water delivery structures and conduct hydrological analyses to model movement of water.
Manage and direct construction operations and maintenance activities at project sites to ensure compliance with design specifications.
Inspect project sites to monitor progress, ensure conformance to specifications, and check for environmental compliance.

Replace hydraulic hoses, headlight bulbs, and gathering-arm teeth.
Handle slides, mud, or perform pit cleanings or maintenance.
Adjust dig face angles for varying overburden depths and set lengths.
Direct workers engaged in placing blocks or securing machines when lifting heavy loads.
Direct ground workers in moving stakes, markers, or changing positions of towers.
Perform manual labor to prepare or finish sites, such as shoveling materials by hand.
Drive machines and heavy equipment to work sites and operate them for various tasks.
Receive instructions regarding material movement or excavation processes.

Move materials over short distances around a construction site or work area.
Lubricate, adjust, or repair machinery and replace worn parts.

Create or maintain inclines, ramps, and perform various drainage tasks.
Erect, install, or repair road signs, guardrails, and highway markers using tools.
Dump, spread, and tamp asphalt to repair joints and patch pavement surfaces.

Discuss construction project proposals with interested parties such as vendors and contractors.
Develop technical guidance or support for various engineering projects, including wind turbines and solar systems.

Recommend or initiate personnel actions, such as hires, promotions, transfers, or disciplinary measures.

Develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures.
